method_card.parent_question: How can chunked/source-span LLM methods preserve source grounding while still supporting useful abstraction?
intended_inputs: same approved source as pilot 004: the second operator-approved source excerpt selected from the local pharm box-trades transcript
metadata-safe source hash and source ref for the selected excerpt
DeepSeek V4 Pro with thinking disabled
pre-recorded prompt and model configuration metadata
intended_outputs: locally computed quote hashes after response review
known_risks: This is a proposed live LLM pilot planning record, not a completed run.
The model may still emit broad segment labels instead of canonical line and character offsets.
The model must not be trusted to generate cryptographic hashes; quote hashes are computed locally from selected spans.
Line ranges and character offsets may disagree and require manual review labels.
A compact locator contract may preserve source grounding while losing broader abstraction.
Prompt or trace handling may accidentally include raw source material if the privacy boundary is not followed.
